channel.title: CrossWire
channel.description: Curated briefings and reports from MOCR — Hermes, Tech, Commanders, and system reports

item.title: CrossWire: Sentinel Census — July 24
item.description:
🟢 Sentinel Census — July 24, 2026

Scorecard: 27 Green · 0 Yellow · 0 Red — A clean weekly bill of health for MOCR.

All systems, services, and scripts passed inspection:

  • Jobs (8): Broom-Run, Scrapbook-Ingest, Mail Call Sync, Scrapbook Weekly Lint, CrossWire (morning & evening), Switchboard, Sentinel Sweeps (Watchdog + Census) — all healthy
  • Scripts (11): All crosswire, scrapbook, mail call, Paige monitor, and sentinel scripts verified present and intact
  • Services (7): Mail Call, Stableboard, Hermes WebUI, Glimpse, Glimpse Funnel Gateway, Stableboard (dev), Ollama — all healthy

No fixes required. Standard system state — keep the Watchdog running and the next Census on autopilot.

item.title: CrossWire: Hermes — Morning Brief, July 24
item.description:
🚀 Hermes Ecosystem — Friday Morning

📊 State of Hermes: July 2026 Report

Hermes Atlas published their July report covering the eight weeks since May. The story this quarter: surface area. Hermes is no longer just a fast agent harness — it's becoming an ecosystem with workspaces, GUIs, skill registries, and desktop apps. Key numbers:

  • 216K+ stars on core repo (+51K since May)
  • 203 Atlas-tracked ecosystem projects (+80), 1.17M combined stars (~3.0x since May)
  • Skills & Skill Registries became the largest Atlas category: 34 repos / 377K stars
  • Workspaces & GUIs exploded from 11 repos / 28.7K stars to 19 repos / 200.7K stars (~7×)
  • The v0.18 "Judgment" release cleared 100% of open P0/P1 items

The report frames the arc as Velocity → Surface → Reach → Judgment. We're running on the result right now.

🔍 Notable PRs — July 24

  • #70260 — feat(version): derive display versions from release tags (ethernet8023) — Unifies version display across CLI, TUI, Desktop, and Nix builds using SemVer-derived boundaries instead of ad-hoc CalVer probes. Every surface reads from the same version_info.py. MOCR relevance: Cleaner version discipline aligns with our ship-version tracking pattern.
  • #71007 — fix(web): resolve per-profile gateway state for ?profile= in /api/status (teknium1) — Dashboard /api/status now reports the requested profile's gateway state instead of always reading the active profile. Relevant for profile multiplexing and dashboard accuracy in multi-profile setups.
  • #71001 — fix(compression): recover rotated session lineage (teknium1) — Session compression edge case fix. A rotated session's lineage can now be recovered correctly during compression.
  • #71042 — ci: add detailed logging to live comment poller (ethernet8023) — CI infrastructure improvement: more verbose logging on the live comment poller for debugging pipeline issues.
  • #66522 — feat(vertex): route Claude models through the AnthropicVertex SDK (nickkpoon) — Opens Vertex AI as a routing path for Claude models via the AnthropicVertex SDK. Enterprise deployment relevance for organizations using GCP.

🐛 Bug Watch — Desktop Session Resume

#70835 — Desktop 'Resume failed' TypeError (DavidMetcalfe, P2) — When resuming a session in Hermes Desktop containing an async_delegation_complete display event, a "Resume failed" error appears: Cannot use 'in' operator to search for 'task_count' in {"delegation_id": "deleg_...", ...}. The display_metadata field isn't being deserialized during get_messages / get_messages_around. If you're using delegate_task in Desktop sessions, avoid session resume until this is patched.

🛠️ Community Builds — New This Week

  • design-judge-skills (⭐334) — Evidence-driven Agent Skills for design award research, evaluation, and entry writing. Week's hottest debut.
  • frakio-work (⭐54) — Codex + Hermes + Arc multi-agent collaboration workspace.
  • hermes-improvements (⭐8) — Vector memory, adaptive soul, workflow classification — architectural enhancement suite for Hermes.
  • hermes-newton-operations (⭐2) — Profile-scoped operations dashboard and bounded decision journal. MOCR relevance: The decision journal pattern is directly applicable to how we track MOCR architectural decisions.
  • agent-console (⭐3) — Lightweight browser mission control for one Hermes agent: chat, sessions, cron, skills, voice, and read-only missions.
  • slashstack-skills (⭐3) — 11 MIT-licensed markdown skills: /preflight, /audit, /ship, /remember. Zero-dependency agent guardrails. The /preflight pattern maps to our quality gates.

📈 Gaining Popularity (Month to Date)

  • hermex (⭐916) — Native iPhone app for Hermes. Up from 913 in the evening brief, closing on 1K.
  • EvoForge (⭐101) — High-performance desktop agent workbench with evolutionary capability.
  • memmy-agent (⭐68) — Personal AI agent & local memory hub for Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, and Hermes.
  • mind (⭐58) — Persistent memory for coding agents: auto-capture, explainable recall, knowledge consolidation, privacy controls.
  • lyric-video-director (⭐43) — Hermes Agent skill for typography-led lyric videos using GPT Image 2, Gemini Omni Flash, and exact-text QC.
  • trajectory (⭐40) — Convert sessions across harnesses to a unified trajectory format for memory formation, dreaming, and search.

item.title: CrossWire: Tech — Morning Brief, July 24
item.description:
🍎 Apple

  • Trump vows retaliation over EU fines — Trump on Truth Social announced an investigation into EU fines against Apple, Google, and Meta for antitrust/DMA violations, threatening retaliatory tariffs. The EU has fined US tech companies over $25B in two decades. The Financial Times reports the EU may preemptively limit Apple and Meta fines to avoid escalation. 9to5Mac coverage.
  • iPhone Ultra: six new features — 9to5Mac details what Apple's top-tier foldable iPhone model will bring. Launch alongside the foldable iPhone expected this September.
  • macOS 27 beta reworks Mail compose — Latest Golden Gate beta shows a redesigned formatting bar that pushes Apple Intelligence Writing Tools and Siri AI into the compose window.
  • Siri settlement approved — The $250M class action over delayed Siri AI launch received court approval. Eligible iPhone model owners can claim payouts.
  • Apple Pay improvements — Four new features announced for Apple Pay, though details are still emerging.
  • macOS Sonoma 14.8.8 & Sequoia 15.7.8 RCs — Seventh release candidates for legacy-OS security updates. Extended testing continues.

🤖 AI & Models

  • Claude Opus 5 launches — near Fable 5 performance at half the cost — Anthropic released Opus 5 today built for daily agentic use. Ars Technica's take: "Models are improving quickly, but the cheaper options are often good enough." Opus 5 offers near-Fable-5 capability at half the pricing — $5/$25 per MTok vs Fable 5's $10/$50. Sonnet 5 actually beats Opus 4.8 on Terminal-Bench 2.1 (80.4 vs 74.6), compressing the Sonnet-to-Opus gap further.
  • OpenAI hardware push + Apple lawsuit — The MacRumors Show covers OpenAI's move into hardware and Apple's trade secret lawsuit.
  • Canadian legislator reads LLM response in floor speech — Ars Technica reports a Canadian legislator apparently read an LLM-generated response during a floor speech, including the telltale line: "Here's a more natural, flowing version of that section..."

🔬 Science & Tech

  • AlphaFold redesigns gene-editing proteins — Ars Technica: Google's AlphaFold used to identify error-prone regions in CRISPR gene-editing proteins, potentially making edits safer and more precise.
  • Most advanced robotic servicing satellite — Ars Technica profile of a satellite capable of refueling, repairing, and repositioning other spacecraft.
  • EU restricts satellite images of Iran War region — New delay on Copernicus satellite pics as US military action in the region ramps up.
  • Judge rebuffs Trump admin on NYT phone records — "We can quash the subpoenas, or you could withdraw the subpoenas," judge told DOJ attorneys.
  • RFK Jr.'s hand-picked committee approves unstudied peptides — Ars Technica: Committee approved manufacturing of peptides with no human safety or efficacy data — peptides the committee chair personally uses.

🏠 Home Automation

Home Assistant feed was unavailable (RSS parse error). No notable developments from other sources this cycle. Standard HA ecosystem churn continues in the background.

📺 Streaming & Media

  • Roku raises streaming stick prices up to 60% — Ars Technica: Roku blames a RAM shortage after the CEO called it "great" for business in May.
  • WhatsApp testing new message bubbles on iOS — 9to5Mac reports WhatsApp is experimenting with redesigned message bubbles in the latest iOS beta.

item.title: CrossWire: Commanders — Morning Brief, July 24
item.description:
🏈 Washington Commanders — Friday Morning

📋 Training Camp Countdown: 5 Days

Camp opens July 29 in Ashburn, VA. Free admission with required ticket registration. Team reports July 28 (veterans) with first practice July 29. Key storylines entering camp:

  • Edge rusher competition — The biggest camp battle. Dorance Armstrong returning from torn ACL. Free-agent additions Odafe Oweh (Ravens) and K'Lavon Chaisson (Panthers). 5th-round pick Joshua Josephs. Daronte Jones' scheme has real options for the first time in years.
  • Ground game emphasis — The offseason's stated offensive priority. Can Jacory Croskey-Merritt and Rachaad White deliver a more involved running attack?
  • Offensive line continuity — Laremy Tunsil anchoring at blindside (ranked Top 5 tackle by ESPN). Interior stability remains a question.

2026 Schedule — Opening Stretch

  • Sep 13 (Sun) — @ Philadelphia Eagles (road opener)
  • Sep 20 (Sun) — @ Dallas Cowboys (early NFC East test)
  • Sep 27 (Sun) — vs Seattle Seahawks (home opener)
  • Oct 4 (Sun) — vs Indianapolis Colts
  • Oct 11 (Sun) — vs New York Giants
  • 17 games remaining on schedule. Divisional gauntlet starts Week 1.

🏆 Around the NFL

  • Tony Romo arrested on OWI suspicion — CBS analyst and former Cowboys QB arrested in Milwaukee Thursday night after poor performance on field sobriety tests. Romo was in town for the Wisconsin Amateur Golf Championship. First-offense OWI under Wisconsin law.
  • Will Grier returns as Cowboys offensive assistant — Former Cowboys QB starting his coaching career on Dallas's offensive staff.
  • Bills pick grass over turf — GM Brandon Beane explained the decision for the new stadium on McAfee's show. Player safety narrative continues to win out over maintenance cost arguments.
  • Patriots training camp — ESPN's daily intel tracking QB battles and potential breakouts in Foxborough.
  • Fantasy football — ESPN's Liz Loza on Panthers, Texans, and Broncos backfield depth chart situations as camp approaches.

📅 On the Radar

  • July 28: Veterans report to training camp
  • July 29: First practice, Ashburn VA
  • Nov 8 (Week 9): John Riggins #44 retirement ceremony at halftime vs Rams
  • Jan 10: Regular season finale vs Cowboys
